MARKET DRIVERS: 1. Brazil export competitiveness declining: BRL strengthened 2.4% this week — Brazilian exports getting more expensive 2. Crowded long in Cotton: Specs net long 56,508 contracts AND RSI at 88 — reversal risk elevated 3. Crowded long in Live Cattle: Specs net long 143,270 contracts AND RSI at 74 — reversal risk elevated 4. Weather premium building in Soybeans: price up 1.8% this week with active weather alerts in India Madhya Pradesh, South Africa Free State, South Africa Mpumalanga 5. Weather premium building in Coffee: price up 3.5% this week with active weather alerts in India Madhya Pradesh, South Africa Free State, South Africa Mpumalanga 6. Soybeans in backwardation (-1.8%): market signals tight supply / strong nearby demand 7. Corn in steep contango (+10.7%): market expects adequate supply, carrying costs elevated 8. Data sources, methodology, and project information

LayerSourceFrequencyCoverage

Analysis Methodology
IndicatorParametersWhat It Detects
Simple Moving Average20 / 50 / 200 dayTrend direction at three timeframes
RSI14-day, Wilder smoothingOverbought (>70) / Oversold (<30)
MACD12 / 26 / 9Momentum shifts and trend strength
Bollinger Bands20-day, 2 stdVolatility compression and breakouts
Crush SpreadOil*11 + Meal*2.2 - BeansProcessing margin profitability
Forward CurveSequential monthsContango / backwardation structure
SeasonalHistorical monthly avgCurrent vs historical norms
Price Units

All prices are displayed in USD per metric ton (USD/MT) for international comparability. Native exchange units (cents/bushel, cents/pound, $/short ton) are converted at the display layer.

Mirror Market is a portfolio project by Philip Bergman. Data sourced from free public APIs. Not financial advice. All data is delayed and provided as-is.
